title: Ethereum Translation Glossary Schema
description: A concept-oriented, multilingual terminology database for Ethereum ecosystem translations. Designed for use by LLM translation pipelines, human translators, and public API consumers.

version.description: Semantic version of this glossary data file

generated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this file was generated

languages.description: BCP-47 language codes covered in this glossary

entries.description: Term entries keyed by stable slug ID (e.g., 'stake', 'proof-of-stake'). IDs are kebab-case, immutable, and never reused.

Entry.description: A single concept entry. Represents one Ethereum concept with all its forms and translations.

id.description: Stable slug identifier. Kebab-case, immutable once assigned. Must match the key in the entries object.

deprecated.description: Whether this term is a legacy/retired term in the Ethereum ecosystem. Use alongside 'note' for context (e.g., preferred replacement). Deprecated terms still need translations for historical content.

note.description: Free-text note about this term's usage, caveats, or lifecycle. Use for legacy terms (e.g., 'Legacy Ethereum term. Preferred: consensus layer'), PoW-vs-PoS disambiguation (e.g., 'Applies to proof-of-work systems; do not confuse with validator in PoS'), or any other guidance that doesn't fit elsewhere.

casing.description: English capitalization rule. standard: lowercase, capitalize at sentence start (gas, block). proper: always capitalized (Ethereum, Solidity). uppercase: always all-caps (EVM, NFT, ETH). fixed: never alter, use exact stored string (zkEVM, DeFi, MetaMask, ethereum.org).

aliases.description: Alternative English forms that resolve to this concept. Each alias can be a plain string or an object with status metadata.

status.description: Whether this alias is preferred, accepted, or deprecated for new English content

note.description: Context for the status (e.g., 'Pending style guide change per content lead')

avoid.description: Forms to avoid in new English content. These are recognized but discouraged -- e.g., title-cased forms of non-proper nouns ('Layer 2'), hyphenated forms ('on-chain'), or outdated capitalizations ('DApp'). Useful for content linting and style enforcement.

related.description: IDs of related entries (e.g., proof-of-stake -> validator, staking)

term_family.description: ID of the root concept in a morphological family (e.g., 'staking' -> 'stake')

script_rule.description: Default script handling for non-Latin languages. Can be overridden per language.

en.description: English source entry with definitions and morphological forms

translations.description: Translations keyed by BCP-47 language code (e.g., 'ja', 'ar', 'pt-BR')

EnglishEntry.description: The English source material for this concept

glossary_ref.description: ID of the corresponding entry in the site's /glossary page (src/intl/en/glossary.json). Null if no glossary definition exists yet. Enables linking to user-facing definitions without duplicating them.

translation_context.description: English-only disambiguation for translators. Not a full definition -- just enough context to translate correctly. E.g., 'financial deposit/collateral, not a wooden stake' or 'computational fuel unit, not chemical gas'. Assumes the translator reads English.

usage_note.description: Note on how this term is typically used in Ethereum content. Helps translators understand register and frequency.

TranslationEntry.description: Translation of a concept into a specific language

aliases.description: Alternative valid translations in this language. E.g., Japanese may have both katakana and kanji forms. First item is preferred if multiple exist.

transliteration.description: Latin-script phonetic rendering (e.g., 'suteeku' for Japanese). Only for non-Latin scripts.

script_override.description: Override the entry-level script_rule for this specific language

reviewed_by.description: Who reviewed this translation (person, team, or 'unreviewed')

notes.description: Translation notes, caveats, or guidance for reviewers

updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this translation was last updated

Morphology.description: POS-based morphological forms. Use null for forms that don't exist for this term in this language. Only include forms that are actually used in Ethereum content.

noun.description: Noun form(s). Singular is the lemma/dictionary form.

negation.description: Negated/reversed form (e.g., 'unstaking', 'de-peg'). Null if none exists.

compounds.description: Compound terms built from this root, keyed by the compound entry's slug ID

ContextForm.description: How a term appears in a specific display context. Contains the term form and optionally an example sentence.

term.description: The term as it should appear in this context (may be inflected, capitalized, or kept in Latin)

example.description: Optional example sentence showing the term in use in this context

ContextForms.description: How the term should appear in 5 display contexts. Each contains a term form and optional example sentence.

prose.description: Inline in flowing text. Often inflected (e.g., genitive/accusative). Example sentence required.

heading.description: Standalone section heading or title. Usually nominative, capitalized.

tag.description: Metadata tag, label, or filter chip. Short, identifier-style.

ui.description: UI elements: buttons, menus, tooltips. Action-oriented.

GrammarInfo.description: Grammatical properties that affect how the term integrates into sentences

formality.description: Formality register (relevant for ja, ko, hi, etc.)

animacy.description: For languages with animacy distinction (e.g., Polish, Russian)

PluralForms.description: Plural forms following Unicode CLDR categories. Not all categories apply to every language.
